reasoning: I base my forecast of 73% on the historical precedent set in 2022, 2023, and 2024, during which European nations consistently made commitments to supply or fund M270/HIMARS launchers for Ukraine. Though active European stocks of M270s are declining, Germany proved in May 2024 that European countries are willing and able to directly purchase US-made HIMARS on Ukraine's behalf. Ukraine continues to suffer combat losses of its existing launchers, ensuring a persistent operational need. With a €40B EU military aid pledge, the continued use of the 'Denmark model' for procurement, and the potential ascension of a more hawkish government in Germany, the financial and political mechanisms strongly support further pledges. The main downward pressures include the potential for a US-brokered ceasefire in early 2025 that might depress new heavy-weapon pledges, and potential export-control hurdles from the US, Israel (for PULS), or South Korea (for Chunmoo). However, given the broad 11-month runway and the fact that a commitment of only a single launcher by any one of the dozens of European countries triggers a 'Yes', the probability remains high.
